schema.appDescription: Calculate exact life insurance requirement using Human Life Value method. Factor in income, dependents, liabilities, and existing coverage.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the Life Insurance Needs Calculator. Smart defaults are pre-filled.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown to see numbers over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust inputs to compare scenarios and optimise your decision.

faq.q: What is the Human Life Value (HLV) method?
faq.a: HLV calculates the present value of all future income your dependents would lose if you died today. It accounts for inflation and expected salary growth. HLV = present value of (future income × working years) + liabilities − existing assets. This is the most rigorous method for calculating life insurance needs.

faq.q: Should I count EPF and PPF as existing assets?
faq.a: Yes. Include all financial assets: EPF corpus, PPF, mutual funds, FDs, and existing life insurance. But don't count your home if your family will continue living in it — it's not a liquid asset for income replacement.

faq.q: How often should I review life insurance coverage?
faq.a: Review every 3–5 years or after major life events: marriage, childbirth, home loan, promotion (income increase). As you age, liabilities reduce and assets grow — you may need to adjust coverage. Review after 10 years of a term policy as circumstances change significantly.
No. All calculations run in your browser.
Yes. Works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: 35-year-old with family — HLV calculation
examples.text: ₹15 lakh annual income, 25 working years remaining. PV of income at 6% discount = ₹1.71 crore. Add ₹50 lakh home loan, subtract ₹20 lakh EPF+PPF = ₹2.01 crore total need. Round to ₹2 crore term cover.

examples.heading: Self-employed professional
examples.text: Self-employed earning ₹25 lakh/year with no EPF/PF: HLV at 35 = ₹2.85 crore. With ₹80 lakh home loan: total need ₹3.65 crore. Irregular income makes comprehensive term insurance even more critical.
